summary: The Two Color Conjecture remains open in full generality: it is unknown whether every orientation of a simple planar graph admits a vertex partition into two acyclic sets. Meaningful partial progress exists: the conjecture is confirmed for planar digraphs of digirth at least 5 (Harutyunyan–Mohar, 2014) and digirth at least 4 (Li–Mohar, 2016), and has been shown equivalent to 2-colorability of all oriented $K_5$-minor-free graphs (Steiner, 2021).

since_posted.title: Planar digraphs of digirth five are 2-colorable
since_posted.claim: Every planar digraph of digirth at least 5 is 2-colorable (vertices partitioned into 2 acyclic sets); the result also holds for list colorings.

since_posted.title: Planar digraphs of digirth four are 2-colourable
since_posted.claim: Every planar digraph of digirth at least 4 is 2-colourable, strengthening the digirth-5 result of Harutyunyan and Mohar.

since_posted.title: A Note on Graphs of Dichromatic Number 2
since_posted.claim: The Two Color Conjecture is equivalent to the statement that every oriented $K_5$-minor-free graph is 2-colourable, providing a new reformulation of the problem.

search_queries: Neumann-Lara two color conjecture planar digraph acyclic partition dichromatic number
dichromatic number planar digraph conjecture 2 acyclic sets vertex partition
orientation planar graph partition two acyclic subgraphs digraph coloring
arXiv "dichromatic number" planar digraph two coloring conjecture proof
notes: The full conjecture (digirth >= 3, i.e., arbitrary planar digraphs) remains open as of 2026. The SIAM page for the Li-Mohar paper returned HTTP 403; the DOI 10.1137/16M108080X is taken from the SIAM URL found in search results. A 2026 arXiv preprint (2603.01020, Harutyunyan-Picasarri-Arrieta-Puig i Surroca) proves the list version of the related Erdos-Neumann-Lara conjecture but does not directly resolve the Two Color Conjecture for planar digraphs.
